name: Current Account
description: A standard transactional account product offered to customers, available for opening through branch channels and linked to broader product documentation.

name: Standing Order
description: A pre-authorized payment instruction allowing customers to schedule recurring transfers, with branch services supporting setup, modification, and cancellation.

name: Direct Debit
description: An authorized payment method enabling third-party collections from customer accounts, managed through branch channels for setup and cancellation.

name: Debit Card
description: A payment card linked to a customer's account, with branch services providing replacement, blocking, and PIN reset capabilities.
context: Debit card replacement, blocking, PIN reset

name: Head Office
description: The primary administrative and operational hub of FirstUK Bank, serving as the central delivery point for extended services including mortgage underwriting, escalated complaints, product training, and fraud investigation.

name: Manchester Branch
description: A selected regional branch participating in the bank's extended services network, specifically authorized to deliver mortgage underwriting and manage escalated complaints.

name: Leeds Branch
description: A selected regional branch participating in the bank's extended services network, specifically authorized to deliver mortgage underwriting alongside Head Office and Manchester.
context: Mortgage Underwriting: Head Office, Manchester, Leeds

name: Fraud Investigation Central Team
description: A centralized operational unit located within the Head Office, exclusively responsible for conducting fraud investigations across the bank's operations.

name: Branch Operations
description: The primary operational domain overseeing all daily activities within a bank branch. It encompasses structured opening and closing procedures, cash management protocols, system connectivity maintenance, and security compliance measures to ensure continuous and secure service delivery.

name: Daily Operations Checklist
description: A standardized operational tracking system and reference document used to enforce consistent execution of branch opening and closing procedures. It serves as the authoritative control mechanism for daily workflow compliance across all branch locations.

name: Automated Teller Machine
description: A self-service banking device integrated into branch daily operations. The entity requires routine physical stocking, balance verification, and end-of-day cash counting to maintain operational readiness and accurate transaction processing.

name: Cash Reconciliation
description: A critical end-of-day financial control process that verifies physical cash holdings against recorded balances. This concept ensures accounting accuracy and regulatory compliance before branch closure.

name: Security Alarm System
description: A physical and digital monitoring infrastructure essential for branch asset protection. The system requires daily connectivity validation during opening procedures and mandatory activation during closing workflows to secure premises.

name: End-of-Day Report
description: A mandatory operational document generated at the conclusion of daily branch activities. It consolidates transaction summaries, system status logs, and cash positions to support audit trails and management review.

name: Staff Briefing
description: A daily communication protocol conducted at branch opening to align personnel on operational priorities, system status, and security requirements. It serves as the primary mechanism for ensuring staff readiness before customer service begins.

name: Cash Vault
description: A secure physical storage facility for branch cash reserves and high-value assets. The entity is subject to daily verification procedures to ensure asset integrity, prevent loss, and maintain compliance with internal controls.

name: Brinks
description: External contractor engaged by FirstUK Bank to provide secure armored transport services for branch cash collections and logistics.
context: Armored Transport: Provided by external contractor (Brinks)

name: Branch Manager
description: Senior branch official granted defined approval authority to resolve cash handling disputes up to a specified monetary threshold without escalation.
context: Dispute Resolution: <£1,000 threshold for branch manager approval

name: Officer
description: Branch staff member required to maintain dual custody alongside the manager for all vault security and cash handling operations.

name: Daily Cash Limit
description: Maximum allowable cash inventory per branch, capped at £20,000 to manage operational risk, liquidity exposure, and vault space constraints.

name: Dispute Resolution Threshold
description: Monetary ceiling defining the maximum value of a cash dispute that can be approved at the branch level before requiring higher-level authorization.
context: Dispute Resolution: <£1,000 threshold for branch manager approval

name: Cash Handling Procedures
description: Governing policy document that establishes mandatory operational controls, security standards, reconciliation schedules, and dispute protocols for branch cash management.

name: Vault Dual Custody Control
description: Mandatory security constraint requiring simultaneous authorization and physical presence of both a manager and an officer to access or secure the branch vault.

name: Daily Reconciliation Requirement
description: Operational rule mandating that all branch cash and transaction records must be fully reconciled at the end of each business day.

name: Cash Collection Schedule
description: Fixed frequency constraint dictating that cash collections must occur three times per week, specifically on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Fridays.

name: CCTV Surveillance System
description: Branch-level video monitoring application responsible for continuous recording of premises. The system enforces a strict data retention window to ensure compliance with security and audit requirements.

name: CCTV Recording and Retention SLA
description: Operational performance target mandating continuous 24/7 video capture and a mandatory 30-day data retention period for all branch surveillance footage.

name: Branch Alarm Monitoring System
description: Physical security platform deployed across branch locations to detect intrusions or emergencies. The system is integrated with a third-party vendor for continuous remote monitoring and incident response.

name: External Security Monitoring Provider
description: Third-party vendor contracted to provide continuous monitoring and alert management for the branch alarm system. Acts as an external control point for physical security incidents.

name: Branch Safe and Vault
description: Secure physical storage facility within the branch used for holding cash, valuables, and sensitive materials. Access is strictly governed by joint authorization controls to prevent unauthorized retrieval.

name: Dual Custody Access Constraint
description: Mandatory operational control requiring two authorized individuals to be physically present and jointly approve any access to the branch safe or vault. Designed to prevent single-point fraud or unauthorized asset removal.

name: Employee Identification Badge
description: Physical credential issued to bank staff to verify employment status and control physical access to branch premises. Serves as a primary authentication mechanism for internal security protocols.

name: Customer Identity Verification Process
description: Mandatory authentication procedure requiring customers to present valid identification documents before being granted access to their accounts. Enforces identity proofing as a prerequisite for account interaction.
context: Customer ID verification for account access

name: Branch Security Policy
description: Governing policy document (reference code SEC-POL-001) that establishes mandatory security controls, monitoring standards, and access protocols for all branch locations. Serves as the authoritative baseline for physical and operational security compliance.

description: The primary physical location and operational base for FirstUK Bank. It operates under a distinct schedule that permits extended staff access outside standard customer service windows, differentiating it from general branch locations.

It establishes standard customer-facing hours across the week, mandates closure on Sundays and public holidays, and specifies extended staff-only access at the London Head Office while maintaining standard customer hours.

description: Tracks the total number of customers at the Southampton Water branch. Current recorded value is 32.

description: Tracks the total number of customers at the Belfast Central branch. Current recorded value is 28.

description: Measures the number of active accounts held at the Belfast Central branch. Current recorded value is 45.

name: Account Opening Time (in-branch)
description: Measures the average duration required to complete account opening procedures at physical branch locations. The operational target is set below 30 minutes, with an actual recorded performance of 28 minutes on average.

name: System Availability
description: Tracks the operational uptime percentage of FirstUK Bank's core technology infrastructure and digital platforms. The target is set at 99.9%, with an actual performance recorded at 99.87%.

name: Branch Availability
description: Monitors the operational uptime and physical accessibility of FirstUK Bank's branch network. The target requires 99.5% availability, with an actual performance measured at 99.68%.

name: Customer Wait Time
description: Measures the average duration customers spend waiting for service delivery at branch locations. The target is set below 15 minutes, with an actual average of 12 minutes recorded.

name: Cash Reconciliation Accuracy
description: Tracks the precision of daily cash handling, accounting, and reconciliation processes within branch operations. The target mandates 100% accuracy, with an actual performance recorded at 99.98%.

name: Branch Manager
description: Serves as the primary operational lead for each branch, responsible for overseeing daily branch operations, managing customer service escalations and complaints, supervising cash handling, coordinating staff scheduling, and executing local marketing initiatives. The role requires proven banking experience, strong customer service capabilities, and thorough compliance knowledge. This position reports directly to the Director of Retail Banking.
context: Qualifications: Banking experience, customer service, compliance knowledge
Reporting to: Director of Retail Banking

name: Director of Retail Banking
description: Senior leadership role within the retail banking division that serves as the direct reporting authority for Branch Managers. This position holds defined accountability for overseeing branch-level operations and ensuring alignment with broader retail banking strategies.
context: Reporting to: Director of Retail Banking

name: Branch Manager Staffing Ratio
description: Specifies the mandatory staffing requirement for the Branch Manager role, establishing a fixed ratio of one manager allocated to each individual branch location. This target ensures consistent operational oversight and localized leadership across the branch network.

name: Branch Officer
description: A frontline banking position located at the branch level, responsible for delivering customer service across counter and phone channels, supporting account opening processes, managing cash handling operations, and maintaining branch administration. The role requires specific training in banking procedures, customer service standards, and cash handling protocols to ensure operational compliance and service delivery.

name: Branch Manager
description: The senior leadership role at the branch level who holds direct supervisory authority over the Branch Officer. This position is responsible for overseeing branch operations and receiving reports from frontline staff to ensure operational compliance and service delivery.

name: Branch Officer Headcount Allocation
description: A quantitative staffing constraint defining the authorized number of Branch Officer positions permitted within a single branch location. This target establishes a maximum capacity of one officer and allows for zero allocation based on branch operational needs.

name: Branch Officer Reporting Structure
description: A governance rule establishing the mandatory reporting relationship for the Branch Officer role. It dictates that all branch officer activities, performance metrics, and administrative outputs must be directed to and approved by the Branch Manager.

name: Head Office Staff
description: The primary administrative and operational hub for FirstUK Bank, housing three dedicated staff members responsible for core banking functions. This office serves as the central point for coordination, compliance liaison, customer service delivery, and back-office reconciliation.
context: ### Head Office Staff (3 at Head Office)

name: Senior Officer
description: A senior leadership role within the Head Office responsible for coordinating daily operations across the branch. This position acts as the primary liaison for all compliance matters, ensuring regulatory adherence and operational alignment.

name: Officer 1
description: A frontline staff member at the Head Office tasked with delivering direct customer service and managing the account opening process. This role focuses on client-facing interactions and new product onboarding.

name: Officer 2
description: A back-office staff member at the Head Office responsible for providing operational support and performing financial reconciliation. This position ensures data accuracy and supports daily transaction processing.

name: AML Screening
description: A core regulatory and risk management concept mandated as a foundational training module for new branch staff, covering anti-money laundering procedures and compliance standards.

name: Product
description: A fundamental banking ontology entity representing financial offerings. Referenced as a key training focus for new hires and subject to quarterly review cycles during ongoing staff development.

name: Mandatory Compliance Training
description: An operational governance rule requiring annual refresher training for all staff to ensure continuous adherence to regulatory standards and internal compliance policies.

name: Product Training Manual
description: A centralized knowledge repository and reference system used to deliver structured product training content and operational guidelines to branch staff.

name: Branch Operations Training Program
description: A structured internal training framework referenced as a key resource for standardizing branch operational procedures and supporting continuous staff development.

name: Customer Service
description: A core banking concept representing client-facing interaction and support functions, delivered through dedicated training modules and reinforced via monthly coaching sessions.

name: Core Banking Systems
description: The underlying technology platforms and digital applications used by branch staff, requiring dedicated one-day training sessions to ensure operational proficiency and system compliance.

name: Branch
description: The physical or operational location within FirstUK Bank responsible for executing branch-level compliance activities, maintaining regulatory certifications, and undergoing annual control audits.

name: Branch Manager
description: The leadership role accountable for ensuring branch-level regulatory compliance, including maintaining annual certification and overseeing the execution of branch control audits.

name: KYC/CDD Process
description: A core regulatory compliance concept encompassing Know Your Customer and Customer Due Diligence procedures, governed by the bank's business ontology and SOP-001.

name: AML Screening
description: A mandatory compliance concept involving the screening of customers and transactions to prevent money laundering, governed by internal policy AML-POL-002.

name: Complaint Handling
description: The structured operational concept for receiving, processing, and resolving customer complaints in accordance with internal policy COM-POL-006.

name: Customer Privacy
description: A regulatory and operational concept focused on protecting customer personal information, aligned with GDPR standards and internal policy PRI-POL-004.

name: Data Handling
description: The operational concept governing the secure processing, storage, and management of bank data in strict compliance with the Data Protection Act (DPA).

name: Annual Compliance Certification
description: A mandatory operational frequency target requiring all branch managers to complete regulatory compliance certification on an annual basis.

name: Annual Branch Audit
description: A scheduled operational frequency target dictating that branch-level controls and compliance measures undergo a comprehensive audit every year.

name: KYC/CDD Compliance Requirement
description: A binding operational rule mandating adherence to the KYC/CDD standard operating procedure (KYC-SOP-001) as a fundamental branch compliance area.

name: AML Screening Policy Constraint
description: A mandatory policy constraint requiring all branch operations to execute anti-money laundering screening procedures in accordance with AML-POL-002.

name: Complaint Handling Policy
description: An operational governance rule dictating the mandatory process for managing customer complaints per internal policy COM-POL-006.

name: Customer Privacy Policy
description: A governance rule enforcing data privacy standards aligned with GDPR and internal policy PRI-POL-004 for all customer-facing operations.

name: Data Protection Compliance Rule
description: A mandatory rule requiring strict adherence to the Data Protection Act (DPA) for all data processing and handling activities within the branch.

name: Branch Control Audit Schedule
description: A governance rule mandating an annual audit to verify the effectiveness, accuracy, and compliance of branch-level operational controls.

name: Document Retention Policy
description: Establishes the mandatory archival and retention framework for FirstUK Bank's operational and customer documentation. It standardizes a six-year retention window across all specified record categories, ensuring regulatory compliance and audit readiness post-account closure or transaction completion.

name: Customer Records
description: Encompasses all archived documentation, identity verification materials, and relationship data associated with a customer. Subject to a mandatory six-year retention period following account closure to satisfy regulatory and operational audit requirements.

name: Transaction Records
description: Represents the complete set of documented evidence for financial transactions processed through the bank. Mandated to be retained for a fixed six-year period to support reconciliation, dispute resolution, and regulatory reporting.

name: Complaint Records
description: Captures all documented customer complaints, investigation notes, and resolution outcomes. Required to be archived for a six-year timeframe to ensure accountability, regulatory oversight, and historical dispute tracking.

name: AML Records
description: Encompasses all documentation related to anti-money laundering screening, suspicious activity reporting, and compliance checks. Subject to a strict six-year retention mandate to meet financial crime regulatory obligations.

name: Customer Record Retention Period
description: Defines the mandatory archival duration for customer records, set at six years following account closure. Serves as a compliance threshold to ensure data availability for regulatory audits and dispute resolution.

name: Transaction Record Retention Period
description: Establishes the fixed retention window for transaction records, mandated at six years. Functions as an operational compliance target to maintain audit trails and support financial reconciliation.

name: Complaint Record Retention Period
description: Sets the required retention timeframe for complaint records, established at six years. Acts as a governance threshold to ensure historical accountability and regulatory reporting capability.

name: AML Record Retention Period
description: Defines the mandatory retention period for anti-money laundering records, set at six years. Serves as a compliance threshold to satisfy financial crime regulatory obligations and audit requirements.

name: Customer Data
description: Customer data subject to mandatory encryption, strict access controls, and comprehensive audit logging to ensure security compliance and regulatory adherence.

name: Head Office
description: The central administrative location designated as the secure destination for daily encrypted data backups and the primary escalation point for security incidents.
context: Daily encrypted backup to Head Office
Escalate to Head Office within 2 hours

name: Incident Response Escalation Timeframe
description: Maximum allowable operational timeframe for escalating security incidents to the Head Office.
context: Escalate to Head Office within 2 hours

name: Information Security Policy (SEC-POL-002)
description: Governing policy document that establishes the mandatory data security standards, encryption requirements, and audit controls referenced in this section.

name: Manchester Branch
description: A branch location identified in the 2026 Branch Expansion & Optimization plan. It is designated as the target site for an Extended Hours Trial initiative, which is currently in the planning phase. The branch will undergo operational adjustments to test extended service hours.

name: Leeds Branch
description: A branch location targeted for strategic staffing expansion within the 2026 optimization framework. It serves as the designated location for deploying a dedicated Mortgage Specialist role. The initiative has received formal approval and is ready for execution.

name: Mortgage Specialist
description: A specialized banking role focused on mortgage advisory and processing services. This position is specifically allocated to the Leeds branch to enhance local lending capabilities. The role carries defined accountability for customer mortgage interactions within the branch structure.

name: 1 FTE Headcount Target
description: A quantitative staffing metric specifying one full-time equivalent (FTE) position. This target defines the exact headcount allocation required to fulfill the Mortgage Specialist role at the Leeds branch. It serves as a measurable operational benchmark for recruitment and resource planning.

name: Branches
description: Physical branch locations serving as operational hubs that currently support a suite of digital integration features and customer-facing services. They act as the primary point of delivery for both legacy and emerging digital capabilities within the bank's retail network.

name: Contactless Payments
description: A branch-enabled payment capability that allows customers to complete transactions without physical card contact. This feature is currently supported as part of the branches' digital integration suite.

name: Digital Account Opening Platform
description: A branch-supported digital platform that facilitates new customer onboarding and account creation through electronic workflows. The system integrates physical wet signature capture to ensure compliance while maintaining a digital-first experience.
context: - Digital account opening with wet signature

name: Mobile App Download QR System
description: A branch-provided scannable code mechanism designed to direct customers to download the bank's official mobile application. It serves as a physical-to-digital bridge for customer acquisition and app adoption.

name: Online Appointment Booking System
description: A web-based scheduling tool integrated into branch operations, enabling customers to book in-person appointments digitally. It streamlines customer flow and reduces physical queue times at branch locations.

name: Video Account Opening Service
description: A planned future digital onboarding method leveraging video conferencing technology to verify identity and open accounts remotely. It represents a shift toward fully virtualized branch experiences.

name: Mobile Payment Demonstration Platform
description: A planned branch activity or system demonstration showcasing mobile payment functionalities to customers. It serves as an educational and promotional tool for the bank's digital payment ecosystem.

name: Digital Check Deposit
description: A future digital banking capability allowing customers to deposit checks electronically. The feature is currently in the planning phase and slated for integration into the broader product suite.

name: 2027 Digital Rollout Target
description: The strategic timeline target set for the implementation and launch of future digital branch capabilities, including video account opening and mobile payment demos. It serves as a key operational milestone for the bank's digital integration roadmap.

name: Wheelchair Access
description: Physical branch infrastructure standard mandating step-free entry and navigation for customers requiring mobility assistance.

name: Accessible Customer Restrooms
description: Branch facility design requirement ensuring restrooms comply with accessibility standards for customer use.

name: Large-Print Materials
description: Printed documentation format specification providing enlarged text to accommodate visual impairment needs across branch operations.

name: Hearing Loop Systems
description: Branch audio technology deployment enabling direct sound transmission to hearing aid users in designated service areas.

name: Large-Text Statements
description: Account statement delivery specification allowing customers to request statements formatted with enlarged typography upon request.

name: Digital Banking Channels
description: Customer-facing online and mobile platforms explicitly configured to meet digital accessibility standards and screen reader compatibility.

name: Accessibility Support Team
description: Dedicated customer service function responsible for coordinating and resolving accessibility-related inquiries and support requirements.

name: Interpreter Services
description: Language assistance provision enabling customers to request professional interpretation support during banking interactions upon request.

name: 30-Day Customer Notice Requirement
description: Establishes a mandatory 30-day advance notification period for customers prior to any branch closure. This operational constraint ensures adequate lead time for service transition and customer communication, minimizing disruption during physical branch shutdowns.

name: Disaster Recovery Process
description: A core operational framework that dictates how banking services are dynamically rerouted to the nearest branch during service disruptions. It encompasses data security protocols and communication plan activations to maintain service continuity and protect customer assets.
context: - Banking services rerouted to nearest branch
- Customer data secured in off-site backup

name: BCP Testing Frequency Target
description: Defines an annual operational frequency requirement for conducting Business Continuity Plan (BCP) testing. This performance target ensures that FirstUK Bank's continuity protocols are regularly validated and remain compliant with internal readiness standards.

name: Business Continuity Plan (BCP-001)
description: The primary governing document and operational control framework referenced for all continuity-related procedures. It serves as the authoritative source dictating branch closure protocols, disaster recovery workflows, data security measures, and communication activations.

name: Branch Closure Procedures
description: A structured operational process designed to manage the transition during physical branch shutdowns. It integrates customer data transfer support, alternative branch assignment mechanisms, and digital services promotion to maintain seamless customer access.

name: Off-Site Data Backup Control
aliases: Customer data secured in off-site backup
description: A mandatory data protection constraint requiring that all customer data be secured and maintained in an off-site backup location. This control ensures data integrity and availability during physical disasters or branch-level service interruptions.
context: - Customer data secured in off-site backup

name: Retail Banking Department
description: A core internal department within FirstUK Bank responsible for managing retail customer relationships, branch operations, and consumer-facing service delivery. Referenced as a primary structural component in the bank's organisational model.

name: Product Offerings
description: The structured portfolio of banking products and financial services provided by FirstUK Bank to its customer base. Referenced as a key domain requiring formal documentation, feature definition, and lifecycle management.

name: Daily Operations Checklist
description: A mandatory procedural framework outlining daily operational checkpoints, verification steps, and task sequencing required to maintain standard banking workflows. Serves as a control mechanism to ensure consistent execution of routine branch and back-office activities.

name: Escalation Matrix
description: A governance document defining the hierarchical reporting structure and approval thresholds for escalating customer complaints, system incidents, or compliance breaches. Establishes clear decision authority and accountability pathways across FirstUK Bank's operational tiers.

name: Branch Security Policy
description: A formal regulatory and operational control document establishing mandatory physical security standards, access protocols, and safety procedures for all FirstUK Bank branch locations. Enforces strict adherence to internal governance and external security regulations.

name: Cash Handling Procedures
description: Standardised operational guidelines governing the secure receipt, processing, storage, reconciliation, and disposal of physical cash transactions. Functions as a critical internal control to mitigate fraud risk and ensure regulatory compliance in cash-intensive operations.

name: Compliance Bulletins
description: Periodic internal communications disseminating updated regulatory requirements, policy amendments, and compliance directives that FirstUK Bank staff must acknowledge and implement. Serves as a binding governance mechanism to align bank operations with evolving legal and supervisory expectations.

name: Organisation Model
description: The foundational structural framework defining the hierarchy, functional divisions, reporting lines, and governance boundaries within FirstUK Bank. Referenced as a core reference document for understanding departmental relationships and operational scope.

name: Director of Retail Banking
description: A named leadership role within FirstUK Bank responsible for the retail banking division, explicitly designated as an approving authority on the document sign-off page.

name: Chief Operating Officer
description: A C-suite executive role at FirstUK Bank responsible for operational oversight and governance, holding formal sign-off authority on the document.
